data.body:
PASS-1 plan review — criterion T1 (Prior-art / novel-architecture justification, overlay): PASS, no findings.

The T1 overlay fires for this plan (it crosses bright-lines: a security/integrity signing integration and a ~50ms performance target), so prior-art consideration is in scope. The plan satisfies T1 affirmatively:

- (a) Relevant prior art is considered / NOT reinventing: the plan explicitly builds on existing in-repo machinery rather than inventing new. It names "reuse rebar's atomic `claim` (re-read under lock + optimistic concurrency)" — the existing `claim_core` (src/rebar/_commands/txn.py:331) — and grounds the fail-closed signing-infra posture as "consistent with the completion-verifier close gate's fail-closed posture." The signature path reuses the documented HMAC-SHA256 signing surface (rebar.signing / src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py; docs/reuse-surface.md) with "no new key custody."
- (b) Novelty justification: no novel architecture is introduced — this is a deliberate, explicitly-named repackaging of established subsystems (close gate, atomic claim, SIGNATURE/head_sha binding). The anti-repackaging / Rule-of-Three concern does not apply because the plan is correctly reusing, not duplicating.
- (c) Capability assertions resolved: the load-bearing claims (single atomic claim operation; fail-closed verify when signing machinery is unavailable; no-LLM/no-network local HMAC verify under the ~50ms target) are each backed by existing implementations the plan points at (claim_core, signing.verify_signature/sign_manifest, attest.claim_gate_check). No unresolved "library supports X" assertions remain.

Affirmations: T1 (a), (b), (c) all pass. Clean chunk for this pass.

(Note: this comment targets ticket cb28-f531-66f2-49cb as the canonical "Claim integration" story; the plan text under review is an expanded revision of this ticket's description with added T9-atomicity and signing-infra ACs.)
